frameworks.name: decision variables / computational model of choice
frameworks.one_line: Single neurons encode model-derived variables such as decision confidence and integrated value

frameworks.name: mixed selectivity / random mixed coding
frameworks.one_line: Authors argue frontal responses are structured, not randomly mixed at the population level

frameworks.name: sparse overcomplete representation
frameworks.one_line: Frontal cortex forms a sparse overcomplete code of task-relevant features

frameworks.name: projection-defined cell-type coding
frameworks.one_line: OFC-to-striatum neurons selectively carry integrated value over sustained timescales

core_question: Do single neurons in rat orbitofrontal cortex encode identifiable decision variables, or only a randomly mixed population code?
key_assumptions: Computational models of choice behavior expose interpretable decision variables
Model-free clustering can reveal categorical response profiles in OFC
A combined perceptual + value-guided task spans a large behavioral space
Striatum-projecting OFC neurons form a meaningful connectivity-defined type
manipulations: Combined perceptual and value-guided decisions in a single rat task
Model-free unbiased clustering of OFC single-neuron response profiles
Mapping clusters onto decision variables derived from a choice model
Identification of OFC neurons by striatal projection target
key_findings: Distinct OFC neuron clusters each match a specific decision variable such as decision confidence
Single-neuron activity co-varies with individual model variables, not random mixtures
OFC neurons projecting to striatum selectively encode integrated value
Striatal-projecting representation of integrated value is temporally sustained
interpretations: Frontal cortex uses a sparse, overcomplete code of variables relevant to its function
Projection-defined subpopulations route specific decision variables to downstream regions
